articles.title: 'Same old story' for Slot as Wolves 'keep fighting'
articles.summary: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot began the week by saying he found most Premier League games no longer a joy to watch - and had little to enjoy as he described defeat at Wolves as the "same old story".

articles.title: 'It's better to be chased' - the mentality of a title race
articles.summary: The Premier League title race is set to go to the wire. BBC Sport has spoken to former winners to find out how players deal with the pressure.

articles.title: Why does Scotland lead the way on heading in football?
articles.summary: BBC Scotland sports news correspondent Chris McLaughlin examines the landscape about the impact of heading the ball in Scottish football.

articles.title: Why do Liverpool concede so many late goals? It is not bad luck
articles.summary: When Andre’s shot deflected off Joe Gomez and over Alisson Becker it was unlucky for Arne Slot’s side. But given that this is a record-breaking fifth winning goal conceded by Liverpool dur...

articles.title: 'The hunger is big' - Sunderland reach 40 points and want more
articles.summary: By reaching 40 points with victory at Leeds on Tuesday, Sunderland have pretty much ensured Premier League survival by early March.

articles.title: Barcelona step up Van de Ven interest - Wednesday's gossip
articles.summary: Barcelona to step up efforts for Tottenham defender Micky van de Ven, Bruno Fernandes future at Manchester United depends on Champions League qualification, Aston Villa keen on Fulham's Harry Wilson, ...

articles.title: Wolves score late winner to secure shock win against Liverpool
articles.summary: Andre scores a 94th minute winner as bottom club Wolves beat Liverpool 2-1 at Molineux to secure just their third win in the Premier League this season.

articles.title: Diarra penalty enough to give Sunderland victory at Leeds
articles.summary: Habib Diarra scores the only goal from the penalty spot to give Sunderland a 1-0 win against Leeds at Elland Road in the Premier League.

articles.title: Bournemouth held to frustrating goalless draw by Brentford
articles.summary: Bournemouth hit the post twice and have a goal disallowed as they draw 0-0 against Brentford at the Vitality Stadium in the Premier League.

articles.title: Wolves winner 'not even a chance' - Slot
articles.summary: Liverpool manager Arne Slot says the late 2-1 defeat by Wolves followed a pattern he has seen "many times this season" with his team dominating possession and conceding from "not even a chance".

articles.title: 'We'd forgotten how to win' - Edwards on 'enjoyable' victory
articles.summary: Wolves manager Rob Edwards praises his sides commitment to improvement, but insists they're not thinking about staying in the Premier League, despite beating Liverpool 2-1 at Molineux to give them bac...

articles.title: Moyes wants players to believe Europe is within reach
articles.summary: Everton manager David Moyes is pleased his side ended their winless home run with a 2-0 victory over Burnley, and says he wants his players to be looking up the table and putting some of the top teams...

articles.title: Tottenham write to PGMOL over refereeing decisions
articles.summary: Tottenham have written to Professional Game Match Officials Limited (PGMOL) chief Howard Webb to raise their concerns over key refereeing decisions, sources have told BBC Sport.

articles.title: NI have 'something to build on' under Lindsay
articles.summary: Northern Ireland captain Jackie Burns says interim manager Kris Lindsay has given the players "something to build on" despite a 2-0 defeat in Switzerland.

articles.title: Farke 'frustrated' as 'dominant' Leeds lose to Sunderland
articles.summary: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says it felt like it was "written in the stars" that his side would lose against Sunderland, after Leeds were beaten 1-0 at Elland Road in the Premier League.

articles.summary: League One strugglers Port Vale cause a cup upset as they beat Championship side Bristol City 1-0 in extra-time at Vale Park to book their place in the FA Cup fifth round for the first time in 30 year...

articles.title: England squad not 'political spokespeople' - PFA chief
articles.summary: England's players are worried about being turned into "political spokespeople" at the World Cup, the chief executive of the Professional Footballers' Association says.
